description: Test corpus for the error-handling-security skill. It is schema-driven because no deterministic gate scanner judges these fixtures — a model reads each snippet against the skill's ALWAYS/NEVER rules; 'detect' means the code leaks internal detail (stack traces, SQL, paths, version banners, record-presence) to the client and must be flagged (CWE-209), while 'ignore' means the secure fix that logs detail server-side and returns a sanitized generic error with a correlation ID.

fixtures.reason: Returns err.stack (full stack trace) to the client, exposing file paths, framework internals and code structure. Violates the NEVER 'render printStackTrace/traceback to client' rule. CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information.
Logs the full error with context server-side and returns only a stable code, generic message and correlation ID. Follows the ALWAYS 'log server-side, return sanitized error' rule. No CWE-209 leak.

Echoes the raw database driver error (e.g. 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint "users_email_key"'), disclosing table and column names. Violates NEVER 'echo SQL queries/parameters in error messages'. CWE-209.

Maps the DB error code to a generic client message without revealing table/column names; logs detail server-side with a correlation ID. Follows ALWAYS centralized sanitized-error rule. No sensitive disclosure.
Distinct 'User not found' vs 'Invalid password' responses let an attacker enumerate valid accounts. Violates NEVER 'leak presence-of-record information'. CWE-209 (sensitive info via error variation).

Returns one uniform message and status for both unknown-user and wrong-password cases, preventing account enumeration. Follows ALWAYS 'uniform error responses / same message for wrong username vs password'. No CWE-209 leak.
